meta.title: AWS Cloud Practitioner Flashcards by cloudwithshad

cards.q: What does AWS stand for?
cards.a: Amazon Web Services

cards.q: Which AWS service offers object storage with 99.999999999% (11 nines) of durability?
cards.a: Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service)

cards.q: What AWS service lets you run virtual machines in the cloud?
cards.a: Amazon EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud)

cards.q: Which AWS service is a Content Delivery Network (CDN)?
cards.a: Amazon CloudFront

cards.q: What does IAM stand for?
cards.a: Identity and Access Management

cards.q: A Region in AWS contains multiple isolated locations called what?
cards.a: Availability Zones (AZs)

cards.q: What is the minimum number of Availability Zones in an AWS Region?
cards.a: Three

cards.q: What is the AWS account user that has full access to everything and should NOT be used for daily work?
cards.a: The root user

cards.q: What should you ALWAYS enable on the AWS root account immediately?
cards.a: MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication)

cards.q: Which AWS service lets you set custom alerts when your spending exceeds a threshold?
cards.a: AWS Budgets (or Billing Alarms via CloudWatch)

cards.q: What is the storage class in S3 designed for long-term archival with retrieval times of hours?
cards.a: S3 Glacier (or S3 Glacier Deep Archive for the cheapest)

cards.q: What is the maximum size of a single S3 object?
cards.a: 5 TB

cards.q: Which compute service runs your code without needing to manage servers?
cards.a: AWS Lambda (serverless)

cards.q: What pricing model lets you pay only for what you use, with no upfront commitment?
cards.a: On-Demand pricing

cards.q: What pricing model gives you the biggest discount in exchange for a 1 or 3 year commitment?
cards.a: Reserved Instances (or Savings Plans)

cards.q: What pricing model lets you bid for unused EC2 capacity at up to 90% discount?
cards.a: Spot Instances

cards.q: What are the three main cloud computing models?
cards.a: IaaS (Infrastructure), PaaS (Platform), SaaS (Software)

cards.q: Which of the AWS Well-Architected Framework pillars focuses on running and monitoring systems?
cards.a: Operational Excellence

cards.q: How many pillars does the AWS Well-Architected Framework have?
cards.a: Six: Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, Sustainability

cards.q: Name two ways to launch an EC2 instance.
cards.a: AWS Console (web UI), AWS CLI, AWS SDK, CloudFormation, or Terraform

cards.q: What is the difference between S3 and EBS?
cards.a: S3 is object storage (accessed via API, virtually unlimited). EBS is block storage (attached to an EC2 instance like a hard drive).

cards.q: What does EBS stand for?
cards.a: Elastic Block Store

cards.q: What is the AWS Free Tier limit for EC2 t2.micro/t3.micro per month?
cards.a: 750 hours per month for 12 months

cards.q: What is the AWS Free Tier limit for S3 storage?
cards.a: 5 GB of S3 Standard storage per month for 12 months

cards.q: What is the AWS service for centrally managing access to AWS services?
cards.a: AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management)

cards.q: What is an IAM Policy?
cards.a: A JSON document that defines what actions are allowed or denied on what resources

cards.q: What is the principle of least privilege?
cards.a: Grant users only the minimum permissions they need to do their job — nothing more

cards.q: What is the Shared Responsibility Model?
cards.a: AWS is responsible for security OF the cloud (hardware, infrastructure). The customer is responsible for security IN the cloud (data, configurations, IAM).

cards.q: What two AWS support plans are paid (not free)?
cards.a: Developer, Business, Enterprise On-Ramp, and Enterprise are paid. Basic is free.

cards.q: What AWS service should you use to register and manage a domain name?
cards.a: Amazon Route 53

cards.q: What does VPC stand for?
cards.a: Virtual Private Cloud

cards.q: What is the purpose of a subnet in a VPC?
cards.a: To divide a VPC into smaller IP address ranges, often separating public and private resources

cards.q: What is a public subnet?
cards.a: A subnet whose route table directs traffic to an Internet Gateway, allowing resources to access (and be accessed from) the internet

cards.q: What is a private subnet?
cards.a: A subnet with no direct route to the internet — used for backend resources like databases

cards.q: What does NAT stand for, and why is it used in AWS?
cards.a: Network Address Translation. NAT Gateways allow private subnet resources to access the internet for updates/downloads, but prevent inbound internet connections.

cards.q: What is the difference between a Security Group and a NACL?
cards.a: Security Groups are stateful (return traffic auto-allowed) and operate at the instance level. NACLs are stateless and operate at the subnet level.

cards.q: What is an Internet Gateway?
cards.a: A VPC component that allows communication between resources in your VPC and the public internet

cards.q: What does RDS stand for?
cards.a: Relational Database Service

cards.q: Name three database engines supported by Amazon RDS.
cards.a: MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, Amazon Aurora

cards.q: What AWS service is a fully managed NoSQL database?
cards.a: Amazon DynamoDB

cards.q: What is Amazon Aurora?
cards.a: A MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database built by AWS, designed for high performance and availability

cards.q: What feature enables an RDS database to automatically fail over to a standby copy in another AZ?
cards.a: Multi-AZ deployment

cards.q: What is the difference between an RDS Read Replica and Multi-AZ?
cards.a: Read Replicas scale read traffic (can be in different regions). Multi-AZ provides high availability via synchronous standby in another AZ (NOT for read scaling).

cards.q: Which database service is best for caching with sub-millisecond latency?
cards.a: Amazon ElastiCache (Redis or Memcached)

cards.q: What is Amazon Redshift used for?
cards.a: Data warehousing — analyzing very large datasets with SQL

cards.q: What is Amazon EFS?
cards.a: Elastic File System — a fully managed shared file system that can be mounted from multiple EC2 instances at once

cards.q: What is the difference between S3, EBS, and EFS?
cards.a: S3 = object storage (HTTP API). EBS = block storage attached to ONE EC2 instance. EFS = shared file system mountable by many EC2 instances simultaneously.

cards.q: What does S3 versioning do?
cards.a: Keeps every version of an object — so you can recover from accidental overwrites or deletes

cards.q: What is an S3 bucket policy?
cards.a: A JSON policy attached directly to an S3 bucket that controls access at the bucket level

cards.q: What is the largest CIDR block (IP range) you can assign to a VPC?
cards.a: /16 (which gives ~65,536 IP addresses)

cards.q: What is VPC Peering?
cards.a: A connection between two VPCs that allows them to route traffic to each other privately, as if they were in the same network

cards.q: What is AWS Direct Connect?
cards.a: A dedicated, private network connection from your on-premises data center to AWS — bypassing the public internet

cards.q: What does a Load Balancer do?
cards.a: Distributes incoming traffic across multiple compute resources to improve performance, availability, and fault tolerance

cards.q: What are the three types of Elastic Load Balancers?
cards.a: Application Load Balancer (ALB), Network Load Balancer (NLB), and Gateway Load Balancer (GWLB)

cards.q: Which Load Balancer type operates at Layer 7 (HTTP/HTTPS)?
cards.a: Application Load Balancer (ALB)

cards.q: Which Load Balancer type operates at Layer 4 (TCP/UDP) and is fastest?
cards.a: Network Load Balancer (NLB)

cards.q: What is Amazon Lightsail?
cards.a: A simplified, low-cost AWS service for launching virtual private servers — great for small projects and beginners

cards.q: What is Amazon ECS?
cards.a: Elastic Container Service — AWS's container orchestration platform for running Docker containers

cards.q: What is Amazon EKS?
cards.a: Elastic Kubernetes Service — AWS's managed Kubernetes service

cards.q: What is AWS Fargate?
cards.a: A serverless compute engine for containers — run ECS or EKS containers without managing the underlying servers

cards.q: What does CI/CD stand for?
cards.a: Continuous Integration / Continuous Deployment (or Delivery)

cards.q: What AWS service is a fully managed CI/CD pipeline?
cards.a: AWS CodePipeline

cards.q: What AWS service is for source code version control (like GitHub)?
cards.a: AWS CodeCommit

cards.q: What AWS service builds and tests your code?
cards.a: AWS CodeBuild

cards.q: What AWS service deploys code to EC2, Lambda, or on-prem servers?
cards.a: AWS CodeDeploy

cards.q: What is Infrastructure as Code (IaC)?
cards.a: Managing and provisioning infrastructure using machine-readable configuration files instead of clicking through the console

cards.q: What is AWS CloudFormation?
cards.a: AWS's Infrastructure-as-Code service that lets you define resources in JSON/YAML templates

cards.q: What is the AWS CDK?
cards.a: Cloud Development Kit — write infrastructure in real programming languages (TypeScript, Python, Java) that compiles to CloudFormation

cards.q: What is AWS Elastic Beanstalk?
cards.a: A platform-as-a-service that handles provisioning, load balancing, scaling, and monitoring for your app — you just upload code

cards.q: What is Auto Scaling?
cards.a: Automatically adjusting the number of compute resources up or down based on demand

cards.q: What is the difference between vertical and horizontal scaling?
cards.a: Vertical = bigger machine (more CPU/RAM). Horizontal = more machines.

cards.q: What is an Auto Scaling Group?
cards.a: A collection of EC2 instances managed as a unit, with rules for scaling up or down

cards.q: What is the difference between scaling policies: Target Tracking, Step Scaling, and Scheduled Scaling?
cards.a: Target Tracking = maintain a metric (e.g. 50% CPU). Step Scaling = scale by tiers (e.g. +1 instance at 70% CPU, +3 at 90%). Scheduled = scale at specific times.

cards.q: What AWS service collects logs, metrics, and triggers alarms?
cards.a: Amazon CloudWatch

cards.q: What is CloudWatch Logs?
cards.a: A service that collects, monitors, and stores log files from EC2, Lambda, and other services

cards.q: What is CloudWatch Metrics?
cards.a: Time-series data about your AWS resources — CPU utilization, network in/out, request count, etc.

cards.q: What is AWS X-Ray used for?
cards.a: Tracing and debugging distributed applications — see how requests flow through your services and where bottlenecks occur

cards.q: What AWS service logs every API call made in your account?
cards.a: AWS CloudTrail

cards.q: What is the difference between CloudTrail and CloudWatch?
cards.a: CloudTrail tracks WHO did WHAT (API calls). CloudWatch tracks performance and health (metrics, logs).

cards.q: What is high availability?
cards.a: The ability of a system to keep running even when components fail — usually measured in 'nines' (e.g. 99.99% uptime)

cards.q: What is fault tolerance?
cards.a: A system's ability to continue operating correctly even when one or more components fail

cards.q: What is disaster recovery?
cards.a: The process and tools used to recover IT systems and data after a major outage or catastrophic event

cards.q: What does RTO and RPO stand for?
cards.a: RTO = Recovery Time Objective (how fast you recover). RPO = Recovery Point Objective (how much data you can lose).

cards.q: What AWS service is a managed DNS service?
cards.a: Amazon Route 53

cards.q: What is a Route 53 health check?
cards.a: A periodic check that monitors the health of an endpoint and can automatically reroute traffic if it fails

cards.q: What is an AMI?
cards.a: Amazon Machine Image — a template that contains the OS, application server, and applications needed to launch an EC2 instance

cards.q: Can you change an EC2 instance type without stopping the instance?
cards.a: No — you must stop the instance to change its type

cards.q: What is AWS Systems Manager used for?
cards.a: Centrally managing AWS resources at scale — patch management, automation, run commands, parameter store, etc.

cards.q: What is AWS Secrets Manager?
cards.a: A service that helps you store, retrieve, and rotate database credentials, API keys, and other secrets

cards.q: What is the difference between AWS Secrets Manager and Parameter Store?
cards.a: Secrets Manager automatically rotates secrets and is for credentials. Parameter Store is free for standard parameters and is better for general configuration.

cards.q: What is Amazon Textract?
cards.a: A service that extracts text, handwriting, and structured data from scanned documents using machine learning

cards.q: What is Amazon Comprehend?
cards.a: A natural language processing service that finds insights — sentiment, entities, key phrases, language — from text

cards.q: What is Amazon Rekognition?
cards.a: A service that analyzes images and videos — detect objects, faces, text, and unsafe content

cards.q: What is Amazon Polly?
cards.a: A text-to-speech service that converts written text into natural-sounding spoken audio

cards.q: What is Amazon Transcribe?
cards.a: A speech-to-text service that converts audio recordings into written text

cards.q: What is Amazon Translate?
cards.a: A neural machine translation service that translates text between languages

cards.q: What is Amazon Lex?
cards.a: A service for building conversational chatbots using the same tech that powers Amazon Alexa

cards.q: What is Amazon SageMaker?
cards.a: A fully managed service for building, training, and deploying machine learning models at scale

cards.q: What is Amazon Bedrock?
cards.a: A managed service for building generative AI applications using foundation models from Anthropic, AI21, Meta, Mistral, Cohere, and Amazon

cards.q: What is the difference between Amazon SageMaker and Amazon Bedrock?
cards.a: SageMaker is for training and deploying your OWN models. Bedrock is for using PRE-TRAINED foundation models via API.

cards.q: Which Amazon AI service would you use to detect the sentiment (positive/negative/neutral) of customer reviews?
cards.a: Amazon Comprehend

cards.q: Which Amazon AI service would you use to extract data from invoices or receipts?
cards.a: Amazon Textract

cards.q: What is Amazon Personalize?
cards.a: A service for building real-time recommendation systems (like the ones used by Amazon.com)

cards.q: What is Amazon Forecast?
cards.a: A service that uses ML to deliver highly accurate time-series forecasts (sales, demand, etc.)

cards.q: What is Amazon Kendra?
cards.a: An intelligent enterprise search service powered by machine learning

cards.q: What is AWS KMS?
cards.a: Key Management Service — create and manage encryption keys used to encrypt your data

cards.q: What is AWS Shield?
cards.a: A managed DDoS protection service for AWS applications

cards.q: What is AWS WAF?
cards.a: Web Application Firewall — protects web apps from common exploits like SQL injection and XSS

cards.q: What is Amazon GuardDuty?
cards.a: A threat detection service that continuously monitors your AWS accounts for malicious activity

cards.q: What is AWS Inspector?
cards.a: An automated vulnerability management service for EC2 instances and container images

cards.q: What is AWS Macie?
cards.a: A data security service that uses ML to discover and protect sensitive data (like PII) in S3

cards.q: What is AWS Artifact?
cards.a: A portal for accessing AWS compliance reports and agreements (SOC, PCI, ISO, etc.)

cards.q: What is AWS Cost Explorer?
cards.a: A tool to visualize, understand, and manage your AWS costs and usage over time

cards.q: What is AWS Trusted Advisor?
cards.a: A service that provides real-time recommendations to help follow AWS best practices (cost, security, performance, fault tolerance)

cards.q: What are AWS Savings Plans?
cards.a: A flexible pricing model offering lower prices in exchange for committing to a consistent amount of usage (measured in $/hour) for 1 or 3 years

cards.q: What is the difference between Savings Plans and Reserved Instances?
cards.a: Savings Plans are more flexible — they apply across instance families, sizes, OS, and even region. Reserved Instances lock you to a specific configuration.

cards.q: What does TCO stand for?
cards.a: Total Cost of Ownership — the full cost of running a system including hardware, software, staff, electricity, and overhead

cards.q: What is the AWS service for migrating on-premises data to AWS?
cards.a: AWS Snowball, Snowmobile, Snowcone, AWS DataSync, or AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) depending on size and type

cards.q: What is AWS Organizations?
cards.a: A service for centrally managing multiple AWS accounts with consolidated billing and policy controls

cards.q: What is AWS Control Tower?
cards.a: A service that sets up and governs a secure, multi-account AWS environment based on best practices
